meta.description: 9 derived cross-tabulations from Liminal raw telemetry — data prerequisite for archaeology.html visualizations

frustration_to_automation_latency.description: Hours between first frustration expression and corresponding hook commit

categories.quote: Anything that is not wired up needs to be wired up
categories.latency_display: ~8 hours (same day — wired into 3 hooks + memory)

categories.quote: this is not at all what we were fucking talking about
categories.latency_display: ~24 hours (next day — session continuity system built)

categories.quote: The Ralph loop, by definition, keeps working until the thing is good
the same fucking problem. I cannot see any of the fucking examples
I literally have two models loaded, so fucking find them
categories.latency_display: ~18 hours (next day — Model Tier + tier-based generators)

frustration_to_automation_latency.insight: Average 12-hour frustration-to-automation cycle — the developer converts negative emotion into permanent enforcement infrastructure within half a day

co_authorship_gap_analysis.description: Distribution of non-co-authored commits across eras — when was the AI not credited?

era_breakdown.name: The Seed
era_breakdown.reason: Kai agent did not use Co-Authored-By convention

era_breakdown.name: The Explosion
era_breakdown.insight: Cursor IDE did not emit Co-Authored-By trailers

era_breakdown.name: Great Consolidation
era_breakdown.reason: Full co-authorship from first Claude Code session

era_breakdown.name: The Quiet
era_breakdown.insight: Total silence — no gap to measure

era_breakdown.name: Multimedia Expansion
era_breakdown.insight: AI attribution is now the default

era_breakdown.name: Dogfood Crucible
era_breakdown.reason: April 1 anomaly — burst of non-co-authored commits
era_breakdown.insight: Gap spike — co-author convention skipped during high-velocity burst

era_breakdown.name: THE BIBLE
era_breakdown.insight: The biggest gap — 50 non-co-authored commits in one day suggests human-driven burst or convention override

temporal_pattern.description: Co-authorship nearly universal once Claude Code adopted the convention
Convention breaks during velocity bursts — the April 1 anomaly

cross_repo_velocity_correlation.description: Daily total output (Liminal + all other repos) and Liminal vs. total correlation

correlation_insights.finding: Negative correlation on peak Liminal days
correlation_insights.detail: Mar 20 (28 Liminal, 0 other) and Apr 1 (53 Liminal, 13 other) show deep focus crowds out other repos
Mar 24-27 (0 Liminal, 25 other) — Liminal silence enables other repos to flourish
Mar 19: 23 Liminal + 65 other = 88 total — the most productive day across all repos

correlation_insights.finding: Pre-Liminal period was entirely other repos
correlation_insights.detail: Feb 22 to Feb 27: 0 Liminal + 9 other — building ICM, voice-to-sculpture before Liminal existed

cross_repo_velocity_correlation.interpretation: Negative correlation — Liminal focus displaces other repo work

session_depth_gradient.description: Messages-per-commit ratio per era — proxy for AI autonomy (lower = more autonomous)

gradient.name: era1-seed
gradient.interpretation: AI autonomous — low human intervention needed

gradient.name: era2-explosion
gradient.interpretation: AI autonomous — low human intervention needed

gradient.name: era7-multimedia
gradient.interpretation: AI autonomous — low human intervention needed

gradient.name: era9-bible
gradient.interpretation: AI autonomous — low human intervention needed

session_depth_gradient.insight: The gradient tells the story: Era 4 (9.9 msgs/commit) was the most human-guided, while Era 7 (0.85) was the most AI-autonomous. The developer learned to trust the agent over time, then re-engaged during the Crucible.

creative_dna_flow_map.description: Creative DNA flow map — which repos fed which Liminal modules. Sankey-ready.

direct_predecessors.name: atelier
direct_predecessors.description: Creative Coding Agent — generative art system with Ralph-Wiggum Loop for self-recursive iteration and improvement
direct_predecessors.readme_summary: Creative coding agent that generates emergent generative art through self-recursive iteration. The same prompt runs repeatedly with changing world/context creating a feedback loop where the agent critiques and improves its own output. Direct predecessor to Liminal — same architecture (Ralph-Wiggum Loop, GUI, CLI, config).

direct_predecessors.name: hydra-creative-agent
direct_predecessors.description: Autonomous creative AI agent — MAP-Elites quality-diversity, novelty search, multi-domain generation (ASCII, MIDI, code-art)
direct_predecessors.readme_summary: Self-improving creative AI combining MAP-Elites quality-diversity optimization with novelty search. Multi-domain generation (ASCII art, MIDI music, code-art). Autonomous, Collaborative, Curator, and Meta modes. Adaptive aesthetic model, safety guardrails, persistent memory with vector store and lineage tracking.

direct_predecessors.name: liminal
direct_predecessors.description: Self-recursive creative coding agent — Ralph-Wiggum Loop, p5.js visuals, Strudel/Hydra live coding, Compost Mill, aesthetic guardrails
direct_predecessors.readme_summary: The code evolves. You curate. The system learns. Generative art system with Ralph-Wiggum Loop for self-recursive iteration, p5.js visuals, live music coding (Strudel/Hydra), multi-model swarm generation, deep collaboration, chat-based creative sessions, and a living Compost Mill for digesting creative material.

creative_dna_flow_map.insight: 17 repos seeded code directly, 13 more contributed conceptually. The strongest flows are creative_coding → generators (9 repos, 3,800+ files) and ai_tools → LLM core (5 repos, 1,000+ files).

quiet_period_inversion.description: The Quiet Period inversion — Liminal flatlines while other repos spike
quiet_period_inversion.inversion_factor: During the quiet period, 25 commits went to OTHER repos while Liminal got 0. This is a 100% inversion — all creative energy flowed outward.
quiet_period_inversion.insight: The quiet period was not rest — it was redistribution. The developer was building mcp-video, DialectOS, puenteworks-site, and CEO_Agents while Liminal germinated. The creative incubation hypothesis: other repos were processing Liminal ideas through different mediums.

commit_message_sentiment.description: Commit message sentiment classified as directive (building), corrective (fixing), reflective (documenting), or celebratory (merging/releasing)
commit_message_sentiment.classification_method: Commit message prefix keyword matching: feat/add/create → directive, fix/correct → corrective, docs/chore → reflective, merge/bump → celebratory
commit_message_sentiment.insight: The project is overwhelmingly directive (building) — ~60% of commits create new things. Corrective spikes in Era 4 (Quality Crusade) and Era 8 (Dogfood Crucible) align with frustration peaks. Reflective commits concentrate in Era 9 (THE BIBLE).

agent_handoff_economics.description: Insertions, deletions, fix rate, velocity per agent with transition timestamps

kai.description: OpenClaw agent (Era 1) - autonomous coding agent running a task-job pipeline
behavioral_patterns: Relentlessly sequential: implement component N, then test component N, move to N+1
Self-verification loops: commits 019/020 both verify test coverage percentages
Test-obsessed: spent 8 of 29 commits on testing infrastructure
Near-zero creative latitude: every commit follows the task-job template
Rapid fire: sub-6-minute average cycle time suggests pre-planned batch execution
notable_commits: feat(task-job-1772343681762-kai-019): Verified >92% test coverage exceeds 80% requirement
feat(task-job-1772343681762-kai-019): Achieved >95% coverage for RalphLoop and >92% overall coverage
feat(task-job-1772343681762-kai-020): Super-testing verification and final cleanup

economics.roi_assessment: High velocity, high output, zero iteration — perfect for scaffolding, useless for refinement

cursor.description: Cursor IDE AI assistant (Era 2) - bulk feature addition and scaffolding
behavioral_patterns: Scaffolding-focused: 8 of 15 commits are 'Add X' - new modules, not refinements
Bulk dumps: initial burst of 10 commits in under 3 minutes (00:29 to 00:35)
Quality pass: single large 'Fix 34-item codebase audit' commit suggests bulk generation then retroactive cleanup
Broad but shallow: touched generators, GUI, TUI, tests, config, docs - everything at once
Deletion-averse: only 1,290 lines removed vs 19,785 added (6.5% deletion ratio)
Stopped mid-day: last commit at 14:02, then silent - likely human switched to Claude Code
commit_messages: [A] Enhance core system with self-improvement loops
[A] Enhance TUI with timeline and utilities
[A] Enhance generators, gallery, and preview server
[A] Add generated gallery and output files
[A] Temporarily remove GitHub workflows for push
[A] Add 4 creative coding generators: noise/flow fields, GLSL shaders, Three.js, LLM music
[A] Fix 34-item codebase audit: security, logic bugs, architecture, performance

economics.roi_assessment: Maximum surface area, minimum depth — built fast, left 34-item audit debt
Slowest but most self-improving — built 26 hooks, 6 memory files, and persistent quality enforcement. The only agent that learned from its own mistakes.

transitions.context: Kai completed scaffolding. Project dormant for 18 days.
The Explosion — Cursor dumps 15 commits in 6 minutes
First [I] commit appears — Claude Code begins sustained collaboration
Hooks system + memory established — the agent begins modifying its own behavior

agent_handoff_economics.insight: Three agents, three philosophies: The Assembly Line Worker (Kai) built fast and shallow. The Landscaper (Cursor) covered everything but tested nothing. The Architect (Claude Code) was slowest but built self-improving infrastructure. The handoff from Cursor to Claude Code was the pivotal moment — it happened at the Explosion and never reversed.

lunar_phase_correlation.description: Lunar phase illumination mapped to daily commit velocity — the celestial rhythm beneath the build
lunar_phase_correlation.methodology: Lunar phase dates sourced from timeanddate.com (Central Time). Illumination approximated using sinusoidal model between phase transitions. Sleep disruption science: Cajochen et al. 2013 (Current Biology), Casiraghi et al. 2021 (Science Advances). ADHD-hyperactivity link: Mishra & Mishra 2025 (Research Square).

lunar_phases.special: Total lunar eclipse visible in North America

phase_aligned_bursts.interpretation: Energy builds toward the Full Moon. The waxing phase traditionally associates with growth, accumulation, and building — the scaffolding burst matches.

A Total Lunar Eclipse marks the start of 15 days of near-total silence. The waning phase traditionally associates with release, letting go, completion — the developer released other projects (GlazeLab, openglaze, ICM) while Liminal lay fallow.

The Explosion begins within 24 hours of the New Moon. The dark moon — traditionally associated with new beginnings, fresh starts, and planting seeds — coincides with the project's rebirth (rename to Liminal, architecture overhaul). The darkest sky, the brightest spark.
The most productive 4-day stretch aligns perfectly with the waxing crescent. Quality Crusade, Conversational Turn — every creative breakthrough happened while the moon grew.

phase_aligned_bursts.moon_phase: Waxing Crescent → First Quarter → Waxing Gibbous
phase_aligned_bursts.interpretation: The anomaly: the waxing phase (normally building energy) coincides with total Liminal silence. But 25 commits went to OTHER repos. The energy was flowing — just elsewhere. The moon was growing while Liminal waited.

The second act traces a perfect waxing arc. 140 commits in 5 days building to the exact Full Moon. THE BIBLE (53 commits, Apr 1) peaks on the Pink Moon — culmination, harvest, completion. The traditional Full Moon associations map precisely to the project's final documentation burst.

three_alignments.name: The Eclipse Burnout
three_alignments.project_event: Project goes silent for 15 days after initial burst
three_alignments.correlation: The most dramatic celestial event of the quarter coincides with the project going completely dark. Sleep disruption research (Cajochen 2013) shows 25min less sleep and 30% less deep sleep around full moons — compounded by the eclipse's psychological weight.

three_alignments.name: The New Moon Ignition
three_alignments.project_event: The Explosion — 23 commits, project renamed, reborn within 24 hours
three_alignments.correlation: The darkest moon phase (traditionally associated with new beginnings) coincides with the project's literal rebirth. The entire second act begins at the New Moon and builds toward the next Full Moon.

three_alignments.name: The Pink Moon Crescendo
three_alignments.project_event: 53 commits — the single most productive day — THE BIBLE era
three_alignments.correlation: The highest-velocity day lands on the exact Full Moon. The entire second act (Mar 19 - Apr 1) traces one complete waxing cycle from New Moon to Full Moon.

full_moon_effects.source: Cajochen et al. 2013 (Current Biology), Casiraghi et al. 2021 (Science Advances)
full_moon_effects.key_finding: Effect held even in indigenous Toba/Qom community with no access to moonlight or clocks — suggests endogenous circalunar rhythm

adhd_hyperactivity_link.finding: 20%+ increase in hyperactivity scores during full moon in sensory-sensitive subgroup
adhd_hyperactivity_link.relevance: Plausible pathway: full moon → sleep disruption → executive function stress → for ADHD-adjacent adults, either paralysis (Mar 4-17 dormancy) or hyperfocus (Apr 1 burst)

sleep_disruption_context.peak_frustration_moon_phase: Both peak frustration days (Mar 20 wiring, Mar 30 dogfood) fall in the waxing phase — building toward full moons. Calmest work (Mar 24-27 quiet, Apr 1 documentation) clusters near phase transitions.

second_act_lunar_arc.description: The entire second act of Liminal traces one complete waxing lunar cycle
second_act_lunar_arc.insight: New Moon (new beginning) → Waxing (building) → First Quarter (decision/crisis) → Waxing Gibbous (refinement) → Full Moon (completion/harvest). The lunar cycle maps perfectly to the project's emotional arc: darkness → explosion → quality → quiet → multimedia → documentation.

chart_spec.recommended_type: dual-axis line chart with phase bands
overlays: Semi-transparent phase bands (new/dark, first quarter, full, last quarter)
Vertical lines at each phase transition
Pink Moon marker on Apr 1 (star symbol)

lunar_phase_correlation.insight: The project's second act (Mar 18 - Apr 1) traces one complete waxing lunar cycle: New Moon ignition → Full Moon culmination. The first act peaked approaching a Total Lunar Eclipse Full Moon and then went dark for 15 days. The three strongest correlations — Eclipse Burnout, New Moon Ignition, Pink Moon Crescendo — involve every major lunar phase transition. The moon didn't cause the build rhythm, but the circalunar sleep-disruption mechanism (25 min less sleep, 30% less deep sleep at full moon) provides a plausible biological substrate for why the bursts clustered where they did.
